'''Kai Pearce-Paul''' (born 19 February 2001) is an English professional [[rugby league]] [[footballer]] who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[National Rugby League]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]] and [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[NRL]]. He played as a {{rlp|ce}} earlier in his career.

==Background==
Pearce-Paul played his amateur rugby league for the Croydon Hurricanes. He is a product of the [[London Broncos]] academy.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.londonworld.com/sport/rugby-league-ashes-test-kai-pearce-paul-5373305|title=Londoner set for Wembley stage as England face Australia in rugby league Ashes Test|date=24 October 2025|website=LondonWorld}}</ref>
[[File:Kai Pearce-Paul.jpg|thumb|right|Pearce-Paul as part of the [[London Broncos]] academy in 2016]]
Pearce-Paul is of [[English people|English]], [[Faroese people|Faroese]], [[Vincentian and Grenadinian| St Vincent and Antiguan]] descent.<ref name="wiganwarriors.com" /> He is the younger brother of former [[London Broncos]] and [[London Skolars]] player [[Kam Pearce-Paul]] and older brother of [[Saracens F.C.|Saracens]] [[rugby union]] footballer Kaden Pearce-Paul. His father Junior Paul played for the [[London Broncos]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/no-helmets-required/2020/jan/27/wigan-warriors-super-league-season-young-blood|title=Wigan kick off the new Super League season buoyed by young talent|first=Gavin|last=Willacy|date=27 January 2020|via=The Guardian}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/no-helmets-required/2026/mar/28/super-league-30-sport-changed-matchgoing-fans|title=Super League at 30: how the sport has changed for matchgoing fans|first=Gavin|last=Willacy|date=28 March 2026|via=The Guardian}}</ref>.

==Club career==
===Wigan Warriors===
====2020====
Pearce-Paul made his Super League debut in round 14 of the [[Super League XXV results|2020 Super League season]] for the Warriors against [[St Helens RLFC|St Helens]] where Wigan went on to lose 42–0 against a much more experienced St Helens squad, Pearce-Paul started from the bench and became Wigan Warriors player #1109.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1587/round-14-fixtures-confirmed|title=Round 14 fixtures confirmed |publisher=Sky Sports |date=22 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/wigan-vs-st-helens/teams/54569 |title=Starting Lineups - Wigan vs St Helens |publisher=Sky Sports |date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/54330327 |title=Super League: Wigan vs St Helens |publisher=BBC |date=29 September 2020}}</ref>

====2022====
Pearce-Paul played 18 games for Wigan in the 2022 Super League season including the clubs shock semi-final loss against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62813693|title=Super League play-offs: Catalans Dragons 10-20 Leeds Rhinos - Liam Sutcliffe hat-trick secures win|website=www.bbc.co.uk |date=7 September 2022 }}</ref>

In December, it was announced that Pearce-Paul had signed a two-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], starting in 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2022/12/05/knights-announce-english-signings/|title=Knights announce English signings|date=December 5, 2022|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63858860|title=Kai Pearce-Paul & Will Pryce to join NRL side Newcastle Knights after 2023 season|website=www.bbc.co.uk |date=5 December 2022 }}</ref>

====2023====
In 2023, Pearce-Paul played in 23 matches for Wigan, scoring three tries.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/NDItMjM3NCwyMzc1LDIzODctLS0tLS0tMzAyNDYtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - RLP|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref> His final game for the club was the 2023 Super League Grand Final, in which Wigan defeated the [[Catalans Dragons]] 10-2.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/super-league-xxviii-2023/grand-final/wigan-vs-catalans/summary.html|title=Betfred Super League - Grand Final - Wigan Warriors 10 def. Catalans Dragons 2 - RLP|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=www.theguardian.com |date=14 October 2023 }}</ref>

===Newcastle Knights===
====2024====
In round 1 of the [[2024 NRL season]], Pearce-Paul made his club debut for Newcastle in their loss against [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.espn.co.uk/nrl/story/_/id/39673450/nrl-round-1-canberra-raiders-run-rampant-newcastle-knights/|title=Raiders run rampant over Knights in 28-12 NRL win|website=www.espn.co.uk |date=7 March 2024 }}</ref>
He played 22 games for Newcastle in the 2024 NRL season as the club finished 8th and qualified for the finals. He played in their elimination finals loss against North Queensland.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-exclusive-newcastle-knights-season-review-adam-obrien-20240917-p5kb9j.html/|title=Brutal reality Newcastle must accept before solving confidence-killing 'riddle'|website=www.nine.com.au |date=17 September 2024 }}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
On 6 June, the [[Wests Tigers]] announced that they had signed Pearce-Paul on a three year deal starting in 2026.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-06-06 |title=Kai Pearce-Paul to join Wests Tigers from 2026 season |url=https://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2025/06/06/kai-pearce-paul-to-join-wests-tigers-from-2026-season/?utm_source=Social&utm_medium=Social&utm_campaign=NRL+Signing |access-date=2025-06-06 |website=Wests Tigers |language=en}}</ref>
===2026===
He made a memorable [[Wests Tigers]] debut with two tries on 14 March 2026 in the 44-16 win over [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=14 Mar 2026|author=Tom Smith|title=Kai Pearce-Paul has memorable Wests Tigers debut as North Queensland Cowboys crushed|url=https://www.totalrl.com/kai-pearce-paul-has-memorable-wests-tigers-debut-as-north-queensland-cowboys-crushed/}}</ref>

==International career==
In 2022, Pearce-Paul was selected for England's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign, and made his debut against Greece scoring a try in a 94-4 victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63439484|title=Rugby League World Cup: England crush Greece as Dom Young scores four tries|website=www.bbc.co.uk |date=29 October 2022 }}</ref>
[[File:Kai Pearce-Paul & Morgan Knowles.jpg|thumb|right|Pearce-Paul & [[Morgan Knowles]] in 2025]]
